hero.headline: SSODL Online MBA in Operations: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Symbiosis International University's fully-online MBA with an Operations specialisation. NIRF #24 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Rs 55,000 work-experience scholarship for 2+ year professionals. Procurement, TQM, Digital Manufacturing and Lean Six Sigma in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: SSODL Online MBA Operations: Symbiosis brand (NIRF #24, NAAC A++), Rs 55K work-experience scholarship, operations depth with Procurement, Service Operations, TQM, Digital Manufacturing Analytics and Lean Six Sigma. Fees Rs 3.15L with scholarship, Rs 3.70L standard. Strongest for operations management roles in manufacturing, FMCG, and IT-enabled services.

about.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Operations is offered by Symbiosis School for Online and Digital Learning, the fully-online arm of Symbiosis International University (SIU), Pune. SSODL received UGC-DEB approval in 2020-21 and is distinct from SCDL.

SIU carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #24 University ranking (2025). The Operations specialisation covers the full operations management spectrum in Sem 3-4: Procurement and Materials Management, Service Operations Management, Total Quality Management, Operations Planning and Scheduling, Outsourcing and IT Delivery Models, International Logistics, Digital Manufacturing and Analytics, and Lean Six Sigma.

The inclusion of Digital Manufacturing and Analytics reflects SSODL's tech-forward positioning. Combined with the core AI and ML for Business Management and R Programming subjects in Sem 1-2, SSODL Operations graduates are better prepared for Industry 4.0 and data-driven manufacturing roles than traditional operations MBAs.

sem1Subjects.name: Business Statistics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply statistical process control, SPC charts, and quality metrics as quantitative tools for operations management.

sem1Subjects.name: Marketing Management
sem1Subjects.description: Understand customer demand signals and market requirements as inputs to operations and supply chain planning.

sem1Subjects.name: Financial Accounting
sem1Subjects.description: Manage operations cost centres, capex decisions, and maintenance budget reporting using financial accounting tools.

sem1Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem1Subjects.description: Build foundational operations management skills: process flow design, capacity planning, and inventory control.

sem1Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem1Subjects.description: Apply procurement contract law, vendor agreement structures, and regulatory compliance to operations management.

sem1Subjects.name: Human Resource Management
sem1Subjects.description: Manage operations team workforce planning, shift scheduling, and performance management for plant and service teams.

sem1Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem1Subjects.description: Design operations research studies, process benchmarking frameworks, and data collection methods for operations improvement.

sem1Subjects.name: Microeconomics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply cost theory, economies of scale, and make-or-buy decision frameworks to operations strategy.

sem1Subjects.name: Technology in Business
sem1Subjects.description: Evaluate ERP platforms, MES systems, and IoT-enabled operations technology for manufacturing and service operations.

sem1Subjects.name: Data Driven Decision Making
sem1Subjects.description: Apply operations analytics, OEE dashboards, and process performance data to evidence-based operations decisions.

sem2Subjects.name: Organizational Behaviour
sem2Subjects.description: Manage cross-functional operations teams, shift supervisors, and cross-site coordination in manufacturing organisations.

sem2Subjects.name: Operations Research
sem2Subjects.description: Apply linear programming, transportation models, and simulation to operations planning and resource allocation.

sem2Subjects.name: Macroeconomics
sem2Subjects.description: Interpret commodity price cycles, trade policy shifts, and economic indicators impacting operations cost structures.

sem2Subjects.name: Financial Management
sem2Subjects.description: Manage working capital, capex investment decisions, and operations cost budgeting.

sem2Subjects.name: R Programming
sem2Subjects.description: Build process analytics models, production data visualisations, and quality control statistical charts using R.

sem2Subjects.name: Project Management
sem2Subjects.description: Apply project management methodologies to plant expansion, process reengineering, and new line commissioning.

sem2Subjects.name: Management Information Systems
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate ERP, SCADA, and operations MIS for manufacturing and service operations data management.

sem2Subjects.name: Business Communication
sem2Subjects.description: Write operations reports, management presentations, and COO-level performance dashboards.

sem2Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
sem2Subjects.description: Translate customer demand signals and service expectations into operations capacity and quality requirements.

sem2Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem2Subjects.description: Apply digital channel strategy to operations-adjacent functions: e-procurement platforms and vendor portal management.

sem2Subjects.name: Design Thinking
sem2Subjects.description: Apply human-centred design to service operations UX, operations workflow redesign, and employee experience improvement.

sem2Subjects.name: Innovation Management
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate Industry 4.0 innovation trends: automation, robotics, additive manufacturing, and digital twin applications.

sem3Subjects.name: Project I (4 Credits)
sem3Subjects.description: Conduct an operations research project on a process improvement, quality, or procurement challenge at a real organisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Governance and Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Apply ESG-linked operations standards, responsible sourcing policies, and environmental compliance to operations management.

sem3Subjects.name: Crisis Management in Business
sem3Subjects.description: Manage supply disruption response, production shutdown recovery, and business continuity planning for operations.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Align operations strategy to business strategy: capacity roadmaps, make-vs-buy decisions, and global operations network design.

sem3Subjects.name: AI and ML for Business Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply predictive maintenance models, AI-driven quality inspection, and ML-based demand forecasting to operations management.

sem3Subjects.name: Procurement and Materials Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design vendor evaluation frameworks, e-procurement processes, category strategy, and materials requirements planning.

sem3Subjects.name: Service Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply the 7Ps service model, service blueprint design, and capacity-demand balancing to service industry operations.

sem3Subjects.name: Total Quality Management
sem3Subjects.description: Implement Six Sigma DMAIC, ISO 9001 management system design, SPC chart interpretation, and quality audit management.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Planning and Scheduling
sem3Subjects.description: Apply master production scheduling, MRP systems, and capacity planning to manufacturing operations environments.

sem4Subjects.name: Project II (10 Credits)
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a full operations management dissertation applying process improvement or supply chain theory to a real case.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Design operations infrastructure for a startup: manufacturing partnerships, production scalability, and cost structure.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Business Environment
sem4Subjects.description: Manage global operations networks: offshore manufacturing, international vendor management, and cross-border compliance.

sem4Subjects.name: Conflict and Negotiation
sem4Subjects.description: Negotiate procurement contracts, vendor SLAs, and cross-functional operations agreements.

sem4Subjects.name: Doing Business in India
sem4Subjects.description: Apply India-specific manufacturing regulations, GST impact on operations, and industrial cluster strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Transformation and Organizational Turnaround
sem4Subjects.description: Lead operations transformation during restructuring, post-acquisition integration, and business model pivots.

sem4Subjects.name: Outsourcing and IT Delivery Models
sem4Subjects.description: Design outsourcing strategy, manage third-party operations contracts, and evaluate IT service delivery models.

sem4Subjects.name: International Logistics
sem4Subjects.description: Manage cross-border freight, customs clearance, incoterms, and international distribution network design.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Manufacturing and Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Apply IoT sensor data, MES analytics, OEE dashboards, and digital twin concepts to smart manufacturing.

sem4Subjects.name: Lean Six Sigma
sem4Subjects.description: Apply Green Belt level DMAIC tools: value stream mapping, waste elimination, and process capability analysis.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all SSODL MBA specialisations. The Operations specialisation adds 9 subjects in Sem 3 and 10 subjects in Sem 4.

sections.whoHires:
Operations MBA graduates from SSODL Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, FMCG supply chain, IT-enabled operations, E-commerce fulfilment, Management consulting (operations practice)

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Analyst, Supply Chain Analyst, Procurement Manager, Project Manager, Quality Manager

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Operations Manager, Plant Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Procurement Head, Quality Head

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, COO, Head of Procurement, Operations Transformation Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Bajaj Auto, Mahindra Group, Tata Motors, Kirloskar Group, HUL Supply Chain, Nestle Manufacturing, Amazon India Operations, Flipkart Fulfilment, Infosys Operations Consulting, Accenture Operations
sections.skills:
The SSODL Operations specialisation builds skills that manufacturing and IT-enabled services employers test at operations manager and COO-track levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Total Quality Management: Six Sigma DMAIC problem-solving, SPC chart interpretation, ISO 9001 management system design, and quality audit management
- Digital Manufacturing and Analytics: IoT sensor data interpretation, MES analytics, OEE dashboards, and digital twin concepts for smart manufacturing roles
- Procurement and Materials Management: vendor evaluation, e-procurement platforms, category strategy, and MRP for procurement manager and CPO-track roles
- Lean Six Sigma: Green Belt level DMAIC application, value stream mapping, waste elimination, and process capability analysis

**Applied skills:**
- Operations Planning and Scheduling: master production scheduling, MRP systems, and capacity planning for manufacturing environments
- Outsourcing and IT Delivery Models: outsourcing strategy design, third-party operations contract management, and IT service delivery evaluation

**Soft skills:** Presenting operations analytics to the COO, building business cases for capital investment in automation, managing cross-functional operations transformation projects, and negotiating vendor contracts.
sections.comparisons:
Comparing SSODL Online MBA Operations against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Production and Operations covers lean manufacturing, TQM, and supply chain fundamentals. SSODL Operations adds Digital Manufacturing and Analytics, AI and ML for Business Management, and Outsourcing and IT Delivery Models, positioning it better for Industry 4.0 and tech-enabled operations roles. Amity is more affordable and better for traditional manufacturing plant management.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management:** NMIMS Ops and Data Sciences combines supply chain management with analytics subjects (IoT, EDA, Operations Analytics). SSODL Operations is stronger on traditional operations breadth: TQM, Procurement, Service Operations, and Lean Six Sigma. For data-driven supply chain roles in e-commerce, NMIMS is more targeted. For operations management breadth across manufacturing and services, SSODL covers more ground.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Operations Management:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000. MUJ Operations covers core operations management subjects at a lower fee with Coursera Plus. SSODL carries stronger Symbiosis brand recognition in Pune and western India manufacturing sectors. For Pune-Nashik-Aurangabad manufacturing companies, SSODL carries a meaningful brand advantage.

fees.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Operations offers 2 fee tracks based on applicant work experience:

**Track 1 — Standard (under 2 years work experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,70,000 across 4 semesters
- Semester breakdown: Rs 92,500 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 87,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,95,000 (including Rs 10,000 registration)
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,75,000

**Track 2 — With Work Experience Scholarship (2+ years experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,15,000 after Rs 55,000 experience-based scholarship
- Semester breakdown: Rs 80,000 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 72,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,70,000
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,45,000

The Rs 55,000 experience scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or opt for one-time upfront payment. Submit 2+ years work experience proof during admission to claim.

EMI is available through the university's marketing partner. This is interest-bearing EMI, not zero-cost. Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options and approval terms.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with SSODL each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and scholarship eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Symbiosis International University (SIU) and carries the SIU name with the MBA designation. The Operations specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from SSODL Online MBA Operations alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Sanjay Patil
items.liked: Total Quality Management and Digital Manufacturing subjects were directly applicable to my plant management role. Lean Six Sigma gave me tools I used in a live process improvement project.
items.disliked: Service Operations Management felt weaker than manufacturing-focused content. Could be strengthened for service sector students.
items.body: I joined from 5 years in automotive manufacturing and the Symbiosis brand was essential for my Bajaj Auto promotion interview. The Rs 55K scholarship made the fee reasonable. Operations and TQM content was strong and practical.

items.name: Pooja Verma
items.liked: Procurement and Materials Management was excellent. AI and ML for Business Management in Sem 2 gave me a framework to evaluate automation proposals.
items.disliked: Rs 3.70L is steep. Interest-bearing EMI adds to total cost. MUJ or Amity are significantly cheaper alternatives.
items.body: Good programme for Pune-Nashik manufacturing belt professionals. Symbiosis brand recognition is strong here. If you can use the work-exp scholarship, the value proposition improves significantly.

items.name: Rahul Kadam
items.liked: International Logistics subject was practical for my import-export operations role.
items.disliked: Limited case study integration in Outsourcing and IT Delivery Models. Lean Six Sigma could go deeper at Green Belt level.
items.body: Decent programme. For Mumbai-based e-commerce operations roles, the Symbiosis brand is less differentiated than NMIMS. Better suited to Pune and Maharashtra industrial sector careers.

faqs.question: How does SSODL's work-experience scholarship work?
faqs.answer: Applicants with 2 or more years of documented work experience receive a Rs 55,000 scholarship off the standard Rs 3,70,000 program fee. The scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or as a single upfront payment. Submit proof of work experience during admission to have it applied automatically.

faqs.question: Is SSODL Online MBA the same as Symbiosis on-campus MBA?
faqs.answer: Both are awarded by Symbiosis International University. SSODL Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved and fully online. The SIU name appears on both certificates. Corporate employers treat UGC-DEB approved online MBAs as equivalent for hiring eligibility.

faqs.question: Does SSODL offer no-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: No. SSODL EMI is interest-bearing and routed through the university's marketing partner. MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI. Factor in EMI interest cost when comparing total programme cost.

faqs.question: Is the SSODL Operations MBA useful for Industry 4.0 and digital manufacturing roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. Digital Manufacturing and Analytics in Sem 4 covers IoT sensor data, MES analytics, OEE dashboards, and digital twin concepts. Combined with AI and ML for Business Management and R Programming in the core, SSODL Operations graduates are better prepared for tech-enabled manufacturing management than traditional operations MBAs.

faqs.question: Is Lean Six Sigma certification included with the SSODL Operations MBA?
faqs.answer: Lean Six Sigma is a subject in Sem 4 covering Green Belt level DMAIC tools. The SSODL programme does not issue a separate Lean Six Sigma certification. For formal Green Belt or Black Belt certification, students would need to complete an additional accredited certification programme alongside.

chooseIf: You want the Symbiosis brand for manufacturing, FMCG supply chain, or IT-enabled operations roles in Pune and western India.
You have 2+ years work experience and the Rs 55,000 scholarship reduces effective fees to Rs 3.15L.
You want operations breadth: TQM, Procurement, Digital Manufacturing, Lean Six Sigma, Service Operations, and International Logistics in a single programme.
consider: If fee is the primary concern, Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management (Rs 2.25L) and MUJ Online MBA Operations Management (Rs 1.53L-1.80L) offer good alternatives.
If data-driven supply chain analytics is the primary focus, NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management offers deeper analytics coverage.
If zero-cost EMI matters, MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI; SSODL does not.
